Computer Science.reality_note: CS is oversaturated at entry level — portfolio matters more than CGPA. Freelancing on Upwork/Fiverr can exceed local salaries.
Computer Science.saturation_note: CS is oversaturated at entry level — portfolio matters more than CGPA.

Software Engineering.reality_note: SE graduates are largely interchangeable with CS in the job market. Practical coding skills and a GitHub portfolio matter most.
Software Engineering.saturation_note: Similar saturation to CS — structured curriculum gives a slight edge for enterprise and government IT roles.

Artificial Intelligence.reality_note: AI is the hottest field globally. Pakistan's local AI industry is nascent but remote opportunities are abundant. Strong math background is non-negotiable.
Artificial Intelligence.saturation_note: Low saturation now, but filling fast — graduates from top programs (NUST, FAST) routinely land international remote roles.

Data Science.reality_note: Data roles in Pakistan are still maturing. Banks and telcos are the biggest local hirers. Remote work can triple your local salary.
Data Science.saturation_note: Relatively unsaturated compared to CS — Excel + SQL + Python is the baseline that most candidates still can't demonstrate well.

Electrical Engineering.reality_note: Government and energy sectors are the biggest employers. Government jobs take 6-12 months via FPSC/NTS tests. Embedded hardware skills command a significant premium.
Electrical Engineering.saturation_note: Moderately saturated — NUST and UET graduates have strong placement records; mid-tier university graduates face a much harder market.

Mechanical Engineering.reality_note: Pakistan's industrial base is limited. Automotive and FMCG sectors offer the best entry-level roles. Oil & Gas via OGDCL/PPL pays top salaries but is highly competitive.
Mechanical Engineering.saturation_note: Highly saturated at entry level — specializing in Oil & Gas, Automation, or HVAC significantly improves employability and salary.

Civil Engineering.reality_note: Heavily dependent on CPEC and government infrastructure spending cycles. Private real estate creates work but starting salaries are low. Gulf countries are the real prize.
Civil Engineering.saturation_note: Oversaturated in major cities — Gulf countries (KSA, UAE, Qatar) offer 3-5x Pakistani salaries for experienced civil engineers.

BBA.reality_note: BBA is one of the most common degrees in Pakistan. University brand matters enormously — a LUMS or IBA BBA opens doors a generic BBA simply cannot.
BBA.saturation_note: Extremely saturated — university brand and internship track record are the two decisive factors, not the degree itself.

Accounting & Finance.reality_note: ACCA or ICAP certification alongside the degree dramatically boosts salary and employability. Pure degree holders face stiff competition from certified accountants.
Accounting & Finance.saturation_note: Saturated without professional certifications — ACCA or CFA changes the employment equation entirely.

Psychology.reality_note: Mental health awareness in Pakistan is rising fast, but formal job openings are still limited. An M.Phil or MS is almost mandatory for clinical roles and private practice.
Psychology.saturation_note: Not highly saturated yet, but salaries are very low — best prospects come from pursuing an M.Phil/MS and eventually building a private practice.

Pharmacy.reality_note: Pharm-D is a 5-year program. The pharma industry (GSK, Abbott) does organized campus recruitment. Medical sales rep is the most accessible first-job route.
Pharmacy.saturation_note: Balanced supply-demand overall — community pharmacy is crowded, but pharma industry and DRAP regulatory roles are selective and better compensated.

MBBS.reality_note: MBBS is a 5-year degree followed by a mandatory 1-year house job. Income is very low for the first 6-7 years. Specialization (FCPS) requires 5+ more years but is where real earning begins.
MBBS.saturation_note: High job security but requires 5+ years of study plus a house job before earning well — financial returns come very late compared to other fields.
